title: Foisted

jokes.text: Fuck!
jokes.setup: Larry is singing Mary Poppins' 'A Spoonful of Sugar' earnestly
jokes.punchline: Abrupt profanity cuts off the wholesome Disney song
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - one word that completely subverts the wholesome setup. Classic Larry David character moment.

jokes.text: I didn't really get an 'After you' vibe.
jokes.setup: Woman confronts Larry about not holding the door
jokes.punchline: Larry's absurd justification based on 'vibes'
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry overthinking social conventions. Good character work but familiar territory.

jokes.text: You have, uh, short hair, you wear a tie, you got a vest. It's, uh-- it's a look.
jokes.setup: Betty asks what 'type' she is
jokes.punchline: Larry awkwardly describes her masculine appearance as justification
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Larry digging himself deeper with increasingly inappropriate observations.

jokes.text: Type plus distance equals no door hold.
jokes.setup: Larry mentions having a mathematical equation for door holding
jokes.punchline: He actually articulates a ridiculous formula
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - turning Larry's overthinking into actual mathematics. Highly quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: 'Fatwa! The Musical,' written by Larry David.
jokes.setup: Jeff announces Larry's new project
jokes.punchline: It's a musical about a death threat
jokes.explanation: Brilliant central premise that drives the entire episode. The absurdity of making a musical about a fatwa is perfect Larry David.

jokes.text: "The idea sucks, Lar."
jokes.setup: Larry explaining his Fatwa musical concept
jokes.punchline: Susie's blunt dismissal
jokes.explanation: Susie's characteristic bluntness cutting through Larry's enthusiasm

jokes.text: The idea sucks, Lar.
jokes.setup: Larry enthusiastically describes his fatwa musical
jokes.punchline: Susie's blunt dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Susie reaction - cutting through Larry's delusions with brutal honesty.

jokes.text: He could wake up in the middle of the night and start punching.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Susie's daughter's veteran fiancé
jokes.punchline: Larry makes punching gestures while warning about PTSD
jokes.explanation: Dark but predictable Larry insensitivity. The physical demonstration adds to it but not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: "Oh. Oh, look at me, everybody. I'm so cool, I hang out with lesbians."
jokes.setup: Susie's proud announcement about Betty
jokes.punchline: Larry mocking Susie's performative allyship
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about performative social progressivism delivered in Larry's voice

jokes.text: She also happens to be a lesbian.
jokes.setup: Susie praising Betty as wonderful
jokes.punchline: Reveals Betty's sexuality as if it's additional qualification
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Susie's performative wokeness.

jokes.text: I had lesbian friends before you knew what lesbians were.
jokes.setup: Larry mocking Susie's pride in having a lesbian friend
jokes.punchline: Larry's absurd claim of lesbian friendship seniority
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and absurd one-upmanship that's very Larry.

jokes.text: I was very constipated. I couldn't poop.
jokes.setup: Larry asks about Mara's two-day absence
jokes.punchline: Overly detailed explanation of bathroom troubles
jokes.explanation: Mild cringe but fairly predictable bathroom humor.

jokes.text: I got married constipated. People do things constipated.
jokes.setup: Mara's constipation excuse for missing work
jokes.punchline: Larry's increasingly absurd examples of functioning while constipated
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Larry's typical overthinking of mundane situations.

jokes.text: I feel like I'm wearing a cement inner tube.
jokes.setup: Describing the feeling of constipation
jokes.punchline: Vivid, absurd metaphor
jokes.explanation: Creative metaphor that's both vivid and absurd.

jokes.text: Instead of sitting in a chair at your desk, put in a toilet seat.
jokes.setup: Larry offering solutions to Mara's constipation problem
jokes.punchline: Suggesting she work from a toilet
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd solution that's perfectly in character for Larry's overthinking.

jokes.text: Bird-like. He was bird-like.
jokes.setup: Larry asks what Richard Lewis's dead parakeet was like
jokes.punchline: Mara's literal description
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan moment but fairly simple.

jokes.text: Sorry about your bird. The good news is I'm still alive.
jokes.setup: Larry's condolence text to Richard Lewis
jokes.punchline: Inappropriately making it about himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David selfishness disguised as sympathy. Brilliant character work and becomes a callback.

jokes.text: I'm fuckin' lamping. What you doing?
jokes.setup: Larry asks what Leon is doing
jokes.punchline: Leon introduces new slang term 'lamping'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment introducing Leon's distinctive speech patterns.

jokes.text: You can keep that boss shit.
jokes.setup: Larry tries to use slang saying something is 'boss'
jokes.punchline: Leon's dismissive rejection of outdated slang
jokes.explanation: Good cultural humor about generational slang differences.

jokes.text: "You can keep that boss shit."
jokes.setup: Larry mentioning 'boss' as white slang
jokes.punchline: Leon's dismissive rejection
jokes.explanation: Leon's cultural gatekeeping of slang terms

jokes.text: Come on, I shot a porno constipated. I ran a 5k marathon constipated. I was in a hot dog-eating contest constipated.
jokes.setup: Larry complaining about his assistant missing work due to constipation
jokes.punchline: Leon's increasingly outrageous activities done while constipated
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation with perfect absurd specificity. Leon's character shines through.

jokes.text: Fucking Jimmy Kimmel foisted that bitch on you.
jokes.setup: Larry confused about why Kimmel recommended a terrible assistant
jokes.punchline: Leon explains the concept of 'foisting'
jokes.explanation: Introduces the episode's central concept with Leon's perfect street wisdom.

jokes.text: "Larry, you-- you never get excited."
jokes.setup: Betty expressing wedding excitement
jokes.punchline: Larry quoting his mother about his inability to get excited
jokes.explanation: Character insight delivered through family history

jokes.text: I have a couple of questions, logistically, about how the whole thing works.
jokes.setup: Betty excited about her wedding
jokes.punchline: Larry approaches her lesbian wedding like a research project
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Larry's inappropriate curiosity about lesbian wedding logistics.

jokes.text: Not really getting, uh, bride vibes from you.
jokes.setup: Betty explains she wants to be the bride in her wedding
jokes.punchline: Larry's blunt assessment of her masculinity
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier door-holding incident with same inappropriate assessment.

jokes.text: That's a fuckin' bride. You a goddamn groom.
jokes.setup: Larry shows Leon photos of Betty and her fiancée
jokes.punchline: Leon's blunt assessment agrees with Larry
jokes.explanation: Leon's characteristic bluntness delivers the uncomfortable truth.

jokes.text: 150 for a bald man?
jokes.setup: Betty charges Larry $150 for a haircut
jokes.punchline: Larry points out the irony given his hair situation
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about the economics of haircuts for balding men.

jokes.text: And there are very few charities that actually focus on the clitoris and talk about it.
jokes.setup: Larry mocking charity name PAM
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's earnest defense involving uncomfortable anatomy discussion
jokes.explanation: Mild cringe humor from Cheryl's overearnest activism.

jokes.text: "I think you got half the population focused on the clitoris."
jokes.setup: Cheryl saying few charities focus on the clitoris
jokes.punchline: Larry's counter-observation about male attention
jokes.explanation: Larry's inappropriate but logical response to serious topic

jokes.text: You just-- you have to say that to yourselves to wean yourself off the relationship.
jokes.setup: Ted saying he and his ex-wife are better friends now
jokes.punchline: Larry's cynical analysis of post-divorce psychology
jokes.explanation: Good psychological observation delivered with Larry's characteristic cynicism.

jokes.text: Sorry about your bird. The good news is I'm still alive.
jokes.setup: Lewis confronts Larry about his insensitive text
jokes.punchline: Reading the actual text reveals its awfulness
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that's even funnier in Lewis's outraged delivery.

jokes.text: I don't live in a Cuban dancehall.
jokes.setup: Larry saying a parrot would be more tragic
jokes.punchline: Lewis's bizarre protest about his living situation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd specificity that's perfectly Richard Lewis neurotic.

jokes.text: Could you come up with a worse name than Soaps On?
jokes.setup: Susie proudly announces her business name
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate brutal assessment
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry bluntness deflating someone's enthusiasm.

jokes.text: What kind of psychopath interferes with the nuances of a lesbian wedding?
jokes.setup: Larry's interference in Betty and Numa's wedding roles
jokes.punchline: Numa's specific and accurate psychological assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect summation of Larry's inappropriate meddling with clinical precision.

jokes.text: I saw wrong and tried to right it. That's all.
jokes.setup: Numa calling out Larry's interference
jokes.punchline: Larry's self-righteous justification
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David self-righteousness in the face of clear wrongness.

jokes.text: That's what Ted Kennedy said about Robert Kennedy at his funeral.
jokes.setup: Larry comparing himself to someone righting wrongs
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely specific Kennedy funeral reference
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd historical reference that's completely inappropriate for the situation.

jokes.text: I cannot tell you how much satisfaction there is in trapping a butterfly in a net...
jokes.setup: Numa sarcastically asking if Larry needs a hobby
jokes.punchline: Larry's oddly intense childhood butterfly collecting memory
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Larry's disturbing intensity about control.

jokes.text: I don't need you to hold the door for me, you prick.
jokes.setup: Larry tries to hold the door for Betty as she leaves angry
jokes.punchline: Betty rejects the gesture she originally wanted
jokes.explanation: Perfect full-circle callback showing the impossible nature of Larry's social dilemmas.

jokes.text: She's a fucking saint, okay?
jokes.setup: Larry describing Susie to Mara as he foists her onto Susie
jokes.punchline: Calling volatile, profane Susie a 'saint'
jokes.explanation: Good ironic description given what we know about Susie's temperament.

jokes.text: That's a foist.
jokes.setup: Leon sees Larry successfully getting rid of Mara
jokes.punchline: Leon identifies the successful completion of a foist
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the episode's central concept with Leon's proud recognition.

jokes.text: Larry David's office. What the fuck is up?
jokes.setup: Leon answering Larry's business phone
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate professional greeting
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Leon's complete inappropriateness for office work.

jokes.text: Lampin'. There's no 'G' at the end. Lampin'.
jokes.setup: Jimmy Kimmel asking about lamping
jokes.punchline: Larry correcting the pronunciation of Leon's slang
jokes.explanation: Callback to Leon's slang lesson with Larry as cultural translator

jokes.text: I did foist her on you.
jokes.setup: Larry confronting Kimmel about foisting Mara on him
jokes.punchline: Kimmel immediately admits to the foist without shame
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing the shameless nature of successful foisting.

jokes.text: "I did foist her on you. I really did foist her on you."
jokes.setup: Larry calling out Jimmy for foisting
jokes.punchline: Jimmy's immediate guilty admission
jokes.explanation: Satisfying confirmation of the foisting theory with Jimmy's honest admission

jokes.text: "By the way, I just did it four times. I started to get denunciation elbows."
jokes.setup: Larry demonstrating ayatollah gestures
jokes.punchline: Larry claiming physical injury from denouncing gestures
jokes.explanation: Creative physical comedy turning religious gestures into occupational hazard

jokes.text: He doesn't like Waze.
jokes.setup: Kimmel asking what else Larry knows about the ayatollah
jokes.punchline: Ayatollah's dislike of GPS navigation app
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd specificity mixing ancient religious authority with modern tech annoyances.

jokes.text: "There's a small harem. Uh, he has a fetish for redheads. And twice a year, he goes to Ireland on a harem shopping spree."
jokes.setup: TV interviewer asking about the musical
jokes.punchline: Larry's increasingly problematic description of his ayatollah character
jokes.explanation: Larry digging his own grave with increasingly offensive material - setup for the fatwa

jokes.text: Larry David!
jokes.setup: The consequences of Larry's Kimmel appearance
jokes.punchline: Actual ayatollah screaming Larry's name on TV
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the episode's central premise. The absurdity becoming terrifyingly real is brilliant.

jokes.text: Even if Larry David converts to Islam, he should still be sent to hell.
jokes.setup: Larry desperately offering to convert to save himself
jokes.punchline: Ayatollah's unforgiving rejection even of conversion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing Larry's situation is completely hopeless.

jokes.text: "Fatwa by association. What kind of fucking question is that?"
jokes.setup: Leon asking about fatwa by association
jokes.punchline: Leon's immediate self-awareness of the stupid question
jokes.explanation: Great self-aware moment from Leon recognizing his own panic question

jokes.text: You know what the good news is? I'm still alive.
jokes.setup: Larry in disguise talking to Lewis about the fatwa situation
jokes.punchline: Uses the exact same line from his insensitive bird condolence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that shows Larry learned nothing. Using the same inappropriate line in his own life-threatening crisis is perfect.

jokes.text: "Tyra Banks dressed up as a fat person on her show to see what it's like being fat. Needless to say, it wasn't very pleasant."
jokes.setup: Defending disguises
jokes.punchline: Larry's understated assessment of fat discrimination
jokes.explanation: Larry's matter-of-fact delivery about serious social issue

jokes.text: Prepare to die, baldy.
jokes.setup: Larry hiding from what he thinks is fatwa-related danger
jokes.punchline: It's actually Betty seeking revenge for the wedding interference
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - audience expects fatwa danger but it's the wedding subplot coming full circle.

jokes.text: Fatwa?! No, cocksucker. Foisted!
jokes.setup: Final confrontation as Larry expects more fatwa danger
jokes.punchline: It's Susie, furious about being foisted with Mara
jokes.explanation: Perfect climax bringing together the episode's two main threads. Susie's rage about being foisted is the perfect punctuation to Larry's schemes backfiring.

tmdb_overview: Larry tries to rid himself of an inept assistant, offends Jeff’s barber and gets into hot water over a new project.
